Method–provenance block
• Property: condensed Fukui functions f⁺, f⁻, f⁰ (Mulliken‐charge definition) and dual descriptor for acrolein; headline number reported here is the largest electrophilic Fukui index f⁺.
• Level of theory: DFT, tier ‘standard’ = B3LYP/def2-TZVP with RI density fitting (PySCF backend).
• Geometry provenance: fixed user-supplied coordinates in /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/acrolein/acrolein.xyz (no geometry optimisation). Single-point neutral, cation (+1), and anion (−1) calculations share this geometry (notation: DFT//file).
• Solvent / environment: gas phase (no implicit or explicit solvation).
• Charge & multiplicity: 0/1 (neutral singlet), +1/2 (cation), −1/2 (anion).
• Software: chemkit fukui-reactivity skill; backend PySCF 2.x.
• Convergence & caveats: all SCFs converged; integrity.status = "ok", integrity.trustworthy = true.  The engine issued three WARNINGS (reproduced verbatim below).

Headline numerical result
Most electrophilic site (atom 4, C): f⁺ = 0.64232102 (dimensionless).

Full Fukui indices (selected atoms, 0-based indexing, 1-based label in parentheses):
  f⁺:  C4 (3) 0.64232102  > C3 (2) 0.43404673  > O1 (0) 0.40136419
  f⁻:  O1 (0) 0.39388616  > H6 (5) 0.18567960  > C3 (2) 0.09140310
  Dual descriptor: C4 most electrophilic (+0.5549512); O1 most nucleophilic (+0.0074780 versus C atoms negative).

Reproducibility
